Label: -1.99E+12
Annotation:
Around 1910, the Free Trade 
#Party and the Protectionist 
#Party merged to form the 
#Commonwealth Liberal Party 
#and oppose the Australian 
#Labor Party.

Label: -1.66E+12
Annotation: In 1916, some members of the Australian Labor #Party left to form the National Labor Party over #disagreements about World War I’s conscription. #The National Labor Party would then merge with #the Commonwealth Liberal Party to form the #Nationalist Party, a united non-Labor party.

Label: -1.20E+12
Annotation: In 1931, in response to disagreements during the Great #Depression, some members of the Australian Labor Party #left and merged with the Nationalist Party, forming the #United Australia Party. The United Australia Party would #form a coalition with the Country Party in opposition to #the Australian Labor Party.

Label: -8.32E+11
Annotation: In 1944, the Liberal Party was established as a #successor to the United Australia Party. It #maintained a coalition with the Country Party in #opposition to the Australian Labor Party. From #this point on, the two-party system would be #Labor vs Liberal Coalition.

Label: 1.38E+11
Annotation: In 1974, the Northern Territory members of the #Liberal Party and the Country Party would form #a new party, the Country Liberal Party. The #Country Liberal Party was a separate party to #the Liberal and Country parties, but was still #part of the coalition opposing Labor.

Label: 1.88E+11
Annotation: In 1975, the Country Party would be #renamed to the National Country #Party. Then in 1982 it was renamed #again to the National Party of #Australia. Finally, in 2006, it would #change its name to the Nationals.

Label: 1.20E+12
Annotation: In 2008, the Queensland #members of the Liberal Party #and the Nationals would form #the Liberal National Party, #which remained in the #Liberal Coalition.

Label: 1.75E+12
Annotation: In 2026, the two party system consists of the #Australian Labor Party vs The Liberal-National #Coalition , which is comprised of the Liberal #Party of Australia, the Nationals, The #Country-Liberal Party (Northern Territory) and #the Liberal National Party of Queensland.
